string.text:
The MAC-10 is a compact and iconic submachine gun known for its lightweight design and impressive rate of fire. Originally developed in the 1960s by Gordon B. Ingram, this firearm gained notoriety for its use by military and law enforcement agencies, as well as its appearances in popular culture. Chambered in various calibers, the MAC-10 is prized for its portability and versatility, making it a preferred choice for close-quarters combat. Its distinctive open bolt blowback operation and collapsible wire stock contribute to its compactness, while its reputation for rapid firing capabilities has solidified its place in the annals of firearms history.

 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple] %c[ui_gray_2] - Scopes: None
 %c[ui_gray_2] - Suppressor: 9mm suppressor
Magazine for the MAC-10 sub machinegun, chambered in 9x19 parabellum, this magazine carries 32 rounds.\n
Magazine for the MAC-10 sub machinegun, chambered in 9x19 parabellum, this magazine carries 50 rounds.\n
